About the Role

The role requires a strong focus on data analysis, machine learning model development, and fraud detection across large-scale datasets. The ideal candidate collaborates closely with engineering and product teams to build scalable and reliable machine learning solutions that support data-driven decision-making. Exposure to model development, feature engineering, experiment tracking, and modern MLOps practices is a strong advantage.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and refine high-performance Fraud Prevention models using Python and Gradient Boosting frameworks (XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost).
  • Manage the complete machine learning lifecycle (data extraction, feature engineering, model training, evaluation, deployment).
  • Conduct data research, behavioral analysis, and performance benchmarking on production datasets.
  • Write and optimize SQL queries for PostgreSQL databases to extract/analyze data for model development.
  • Utilize MLflow for experiment tracking, model versioning, and reproducibility.
  • Maintain code integrity using Git and Bitbucket.
  • Automate workflows with Linux/Bash scripting.
  • Develop data visualizations using tools like Plotly, Matplotlib, or Seaborn.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to improve model performance and decision-making.
  • Ensure data privacy, security, and compliance best practices.

Preferred Skills

  • 5–8 years in Data Science, Machine Learning, or related roles.
  • 3–5 years of Python-based data science experience.
  • Proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) and Gradient Boosting frameworks.
  • 1–2 years with XGBoost, LightGBM, or CatBoost.
  • Strong SQL/PostgreSQL skills.
  • Hands-on MLflow experience.
  • Jupyter Notebook/JupyterHub proficiency.
  • Git/Bitbucket version control expertise.
  • Linux/Unix environment familiarity with Bash scripting.
  • Knowledge of classification, anomaly detection, and feature engineering.
  • Data visualization tools (Plotly, Matplotlib, Seaborn).
  • Analytical thinking, problem-solving, and attention to detail.
  • Strong communication/collaboration skills.

Added Advantage

  • Experience with Spark/Dask for big data.
  • Background in Fintech, Banking, or Cybersecurity.
  • MLOps concepts (model deployment/monitoring).
  • Familiarity with Conda/Pip for package management.
  • Data privacy/security best practices.

Application Instructions: Send your resume to careers@polussolutions.com with the subject line: Data Scientist - [Your Name].
summary: Develop fraud prevention models using machine learning, collaborate with engineering teams, and ensure data security. Requires 5–8 years of data science experience with Python and MLflow expertise.
